---
{"dg-publish":true,"permalink":"/all-published-notes/homelab/011-dealing-with-dns-issues-no-internet-on-new-lxc/"}
---

created: 2026-05-16
updated: 2026-07-15

A common problem is that Proxmox host has it's own DNS address from Netbird, the host machine's config is set to Netbird alone. Meaning any new LXC which is started may not be able to use internet unless they are also connected to netbird.

```sh
root@pve:~# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Generated by NetBird
# The original file can be restored from /etc/resolv.conf.original.netbird

search netbird.cloud
nameserver 100.77.214.80
root@pve:~# s
```

As I installed Netbird on all machine, this is not an issue for the moment, but it can lead to issues later. A simpler fix would be to set a fallback in my host so that if Netbird DNS fails, the LXCs and CTs can still reach the internet.

for this, we will install `systemd-resolved` package which can be used to define a fallback for our `/etc/resolv.conf` which is configured by netbird:
```sh
root@pve:~# apt install systemd-resolved
```

update `/etc/systemd/resolved.conf` to add a fallback to existing setting:
```
FallbackDNS=1.1.1.1 8.8.8.8 9.9.9.9
```

once `/etc/systemd/resolved.conf` is updated to define the fallback, we now want to link our `/etc/resolv.conf` file to this file so that direct changes in `/etc/resolv.conf` do not impact the LXC later:
```sh
root@pve:~# sudo ln -rsf /run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf /etc/resolv.conf
```

This command creates a soft link from `/etc/resolv.conf` to `/etc/systemd/resolved.conf` file so anyone trying to read the source ends us reading the destination, similar to `which editor`. Now, we cannot directly edit `/etc/resolv.conf` and netbird will set the DNS setting as needed but the system also has a fallback for any LXC that does not connect to the Netbird account.

```sh
root@pve:~# ls -al /etc/systemd/resolved.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1567 May 16 12:36 /etc/systemd/resolved.conf
root@pve:~# ln -rsf /run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf /etc/resolv.conf
root@pve:~# ls -al /etc/resolv.conf
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 39 May 16 13:09 /etc/resolv.conf -> ../run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf
root@pve:~# 
```

Now, we can restart the daemons to use the updated setting:
```sh
root@pve:~# netbird down
Disconnected
root@pve:~# netbird up --allow-server-ssh --enable-ssh-root --disable-dns
^C
root@pve:~# systemctl restart systemd-resolved
root@pve:~# systemctl restart netbird
root@pve:~# netbird status
OS: linux/amd64
Daemon version: 0.68.3
CLI version: 0.68.3
Profile: default
Management: Connected
Signal: Connected
Relays: 4/4 Available
Nameservers: 1/1 Available
FQDN: proxmox-host.netbird.cloud
NetBird IP: 100.77.214.80/16
Interface type: Kernel
Quantum resistance: false
Lazy connection: false
SSH Server: Disabled
Networks: -
Peers count: 2/8 Connected
root@pve:~# 
```

> [!Info]
> In case the LXC still has issues connecting to internet, use the same option as host to define the fallback in the LXC.

Another option is when making the LXC, add DNS as sometimes the DNS doesn't work when using host setting (My host uses netbird, whereas the LXC will not be using it when newly created).
